unlicensed_note: Not licensed for children <18 years; used off-label in pediatric hypertension refractory to first-line agents

formulations.indian_conc_note: 20 mg tablet
40 mg tablet
80 mg tablet

dosing_bands.duration_note: Once daily; titrate every 2-4 weeks based on BP response
dosing_bands.notes: Off-label use; start low 0.5-1 mg/kg OD; limited pediatric data; adolescents may use 20-40 mg OD

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 20 mg OD, titrate to 40-80 mg OD as needed
dosing_bands.notes: Adolescents >=12 years; adult dosing may apply

hepatic_note: Reduce dose in hepatic impairment; maximum 40 mg/day in severe hepatic disease; primarily hepatically eliminated
contraindications: Concomitant use with aliskiren in diabetics

interactions.effect: Dual RAAS blockade increases risk of hyperkalaemia, renal impairment, and hypotension
Increased risk of hyperkalaemia — monitor K+ closely
May reduce antihypertensive effect and worsen renal function
Increases lithium levels — monitor lithium concentration

black_box_warnings: Drugs that act on the renin-angiotensin system can cause fetal injury and death when used during pregnancy — discontinue immediately if pregnancy detected
pediatric_specific_warnings: Limited pediatric safety and efficacy data
Avoid in neonates and infants — risk of profound hypotension and renal failure
Contraindicated in pregnancy — counsel adolescent females
food_instructions: May be taken with or without food
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C in original blister pack; protect from moisture
lactation_note: Not recommended — ARBs may be excreted in breast milk; potential neonatal hypotension

unlicensed_note: Fixed-dose combination not licensed for pediatric use; individual components may be used off-label in children

formulations.indian_conc_note: Telmisartan 40 mg + Amlodipine 5 mg
Telmisartan 80 mg + Amlodipine 5 mg

dosing_bands.duration_note: Once daily; use only when monotherapy is inadequate; prefer individual components for dose titration
dosing_bands.notes: Off-label in adolescents; FDC only after stable on individual components; not recommended in younger children

hepatic_note: Amlodipine clearance reduced in hepatic impairment — start with lower dose; telmisartan max 40 mg in severe hepatic disease

interactions.effect: Dual RAAS blockade — risk of hyperkalaemia and renal impairment
May increase amlodipine levels
Amlodipine increases simvastatin exposure — limit simvastatin to 20 mg/day

black_box_warnings: Drugs acting on RAAS can cause fetal injury and death — discontinue if pregnancy detected
pediatric_specific_warnings: FDC not studied in children — use individual components for dose flexibility
Avoid in children <6 years
food_instructions: May be taken with or without food
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; protect from moisture and light
lactation_note: Not recommended — both components may be excreted in breast milk

drug_class: ARB + Thiazide diuretic + Calcium channel blocker triple combination
unlicensed_note: Triple FDC not licensed for pediatric use; use individual components separately in children with resistant hypertension

formulations.indian_conc_note: Telmisartan 40 mg + HCTZ 12.5 mg + Amlodipine 5 mg
Telmisartan 80 mg + HCTZ 12.5 mg + Amlodipine 5 mg

dosing_bands.duration_note: Once daily; only after failure of dual therapy; specialist use only in adolescents
dosing_bands.notes: Not recommended in children; adolescent use only under specialist supervision with resistant hypertension

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: HCTZ ineffective when GFR <30 mL/min; avoid triple combination

hepatic_note: Avoid in severe hepatic impairment; reduced clearance of all three components

interactions.effect: Dual RAAS blockade — avoid
Both telmisartan and HCTZ increase lithium levels — monitor closely or avoid
HCTZ-induced hypokalaemia potentiates digoxin toxicity
Reduced antihypertensive effect; increased renal risk

black_box_warnings: Drugs acting on RAAS can cause fetal injury and death — discontinue if pregnancy detected
pediatric_specific_warnings: Triple FDC not studied or recommended in children
Use individual components under pediatric nephrology supervision only
Monitor electrolytes frequently — risk of hypokalaemia and hyponatraemia with HCTZ
food_instructions: May be taken with or without food; take in the morning to avoid nocturia
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; protect from moisture
lactation_note: HCTZ excreted in breast milk; not recommended
snomed_display: Telmisartan and hydrochlorothiazide and amlodipine (medicinal product)

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for children; used off-label in pediatric variceal bleeding and hepatorenal syndrome under specialist supervision

formulations.indian_conc_note: 1 mg lyophilised powder for reconstitution; equivalent to terlipressin acetate

dosing_bands.duration_note: 20 mcg/kg IV bolus every 4-6 hours for up to 72 hours; max 1 mg/dose for <50 kg, 2 mg/dose for >50 kg
dosing_bands.notes: Administer as slow IV bolus over 1 minute; PICU/HDU setting mandatory; continuous infusion 20 mcg/kg/hr is an alternative

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10-20 mcg/kg IV every 4-6 hours; combine with albumin; continue until creatinine improves or max 14 days
dosing_bands.notes: Pediatric hepatology specialist use only; combine with IV albumin 1 g/kg on day 1, then 20-40 g/day

hepatic_note: Used therapeutically in hepatic disease; no dose adjustment needed for hepatic impairment itself

interactions.effect: Additive bradycardia and hypotension; use with caution
Terlipressin may cause QT prolongation — additive risk with other QT-prolonging drugs

black_box_warnings: Risk of serious or fatal ischaemic events (myocardial, mesenteric, peripheral) — use in monitored setting only
pediatric_specific_warnings: PICU/HDU setting mandatory
Risk of hyponatraemia with seizures in children
Monitor for peripheral ischaemia (fingers, toes, skin) — especially neonates
Limited pediatric pharmacokinetic data

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute 1 mg vial with 5 mL NS
administration.dilution: May further dilute in NS for continuous infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Bolus: slow IV push over 1 minute; Infusion: 20 mcg/kg/hr in NS
administration.storage: Store below 25°C; use reconstituted solution within 24 hours

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light; reconstituted solution stable 24 hours at 2-8°C
lactation_note: Unknown excretion in breast milk; avoid due to vasoconstrictor effects
reference_source: ESPGHAN Guidelines on Variceal Bleeding in Children

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all age groups including infants (usually given as DPT/Td combination; monovalent TT for booster/wound prophylaxis)

formulations.indian_conc_note: 5 Lf purified tetanus toxoid adsorbed on aluminium phosphate per 0.5 mL dose

dosing_bands.indication: Primary immunisation (if not given as DPT)
dosing_bands.duration_note: 3 doses at 6, 10, 14 weeks as part of DPT schedule; TT monovalent rarely used for primary series
dosing_bands.notes: Preferably given as part of DPT/DTwP/DTaP combination; monovalent TT for booster or wound prophylaxis

dosing_bands.duration_note: Single booster dose IM; for wound prophylaxis: give if >5 years since last dose (clean wound) or >3 years (dirty wound); add TIG for high-risk wounds in unimmunised
dosing_bands.notes: Booster at 5 years (DPT) and 10 years (Td) per IAP schedule; wound prophylaxis — add TIG 250 IU IM if incomplete/unknown immunisation

contraindications: Severe allergic reaction to previous dose
History of Guillain-Barré syndrome within 6 weeks of prior tetanus vaccination

interactions.effect: May reduce immune response to vaccine; administer when possible before starting immunosuppression

pediatric_specific_warnings: Anterolateral thigh for infants; deltoid for older children
May cause local pain, swelling, fever for 1-2 days
Severe reactions (Arthus-type) if boosted too frequently

administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with other vaccines in same syringe unless specified
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C; do NOT freeze — freezing destroys vaccine potency

storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C (cold chain); do NOT freeze; protect from light; discard if frozen
lactation_note: Safe during lactation; recommended for maternal immunisation
reference_source: WHO Position Paper on Tetanus Vaccines

unlicensed_note: Deriphyllin widely used in Indian pediatric practice; etophylline component provides rapid onset while theophylline provides sustained bronchodilation

formulations.indian_conc_note: Theophylline 23 mg + Etophylline 77 mg (total 100 mg methylxanthine)
Theophylline 25.3 mg + Etophylline 84.7 mg per 2 mL ampoule (total 110 mg)
Theophylline 23 mg + Etophylline 77 mg per 5 mL

dosing_bands.duration_note: 3-5 mg/kg/dose of total methylxanthine content TDS; based on theophylline equivalent
dosing_bands.notes: Dose based on total methylxanthine content; narrow therapeutic index — monitor for toxicity (nausea, tachycardia, seizures); IV dose given slowly over 20 minutes

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading dose 5 mg/kg IV over 20 min (if not on oral theophylline), then 3-5 mg/kg IV every 8 hours
dosing_bands.notes: IV: slow infusion over 20 minutes — rapid IV causes arrhythmias; omit loading dose if patient already on theophylline; check serum theophylline level if available

hepatic_note: Reduce dose by 50% in hepatic impairment; theophylline cleared by liver — increased risk of toxicity

interactions.effect: Inhibits theophylline metabolism — risk of toxicity; reduce dose by 25-50%
Inhibits theophylline clearance — use ranitidine or omeprazole instead
Induces theophylline metabolism — may need dose increase
Significantly inhibits theophylline metabolism — reduce theophylline dose by 30-50%

monitoring_parameters: Signs of toxicity (nausea, vomiting, tremors, seizures)
black_box_warnings: Narrow therapeutic index — serum level monitoring recommended especially in children; toxicity can cause fatal seizures and arrhythmias
pediatric_specific_warnings: Narrow therapeutic index — children metabolise theophylline faster than adults
Fever and viral infections reduce clearance — risk of toxicity
Seizures may occur without warning at toxic levels
Not first-line for asthma — use inhaled bronchodilators first

administration.dilution: Dilute in 50-100 mL NS or D5W
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse over minimum 20 minutes; never give as rapid IV push
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS and D5W; avoid mixing with other drugs

food_instructions: Take after food to reduce GI irritation
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; protect from moisture
lactation_note: Theophylline excreted in breast milk — may cause irritability in infant; use with caution

unlicensed_note: Licensed for induction of anaesthesia in children; off-label for refractory status epilepticus and ICP management

formulations.indian_conc_note: Reconstitute with 20 mL SWFI to give 25 mg/mL (2.5% solution)
Reconstitute with 40 mL SWFI to give 25 mg/mL (2.5% solution)

dosing_bands.duration_note: Single dose IV for induction; 3-5 mg/kg slow IV over 10-15 seconds; titrate to effect
dosing_bands.notes: Administer as 2.5% solution IV slowly; test dose 25-75 mg first to assess sensitivity; neonates/infants: 3-4 mg/kg; older children: 5-6 mg/kg

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2-4 mg/kg IV; neonates more sensitive due to reduced protein binding and immature metabolism
dosing_bands.notes: Use 1% solution (10 mg/mL) for neonates; extreme caution — apnoea and hypotension likely

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading: 2-5 mg/kg IV bolus; maintenance: 1-5 mg/kg/hr continuous infusion; titrate to burst suppression on EEG; PICU only
dosing_bands.notes: Third-line agent for refractory SE; requires mechanical ventilation and continuous EEG monitoring; propofol infusion syndrome risk if used alongside propofol

hepatic_note: Reduce dose in hepatic impairment — prolonged duration of action due to reduced metabolism
contraindications: Airway obstruction / inability to maintain airway

interactions.effect: Profound respiratory depression — reduce doses of both drugs
Unpredictable effects on phenytoin levels; both are protein-bound
Thiopental at alkaline pH may precipitate with acidic suxamethonium — flush line between drugs

black_box_warnings: Intra-arterial injection causes severe vasospasm, thrombosis, and gangrene — use with extreme care; verify IV access before injection
Respiratory depression and apnoea — equipment for ventilation must be immediately available
pediatric_specific_warnings: Neonates and infants: lower doses needed due to immature BBB and reduced protein binding
Apnoea common — always have bag-mask ventilation ready
Extravasation causes severe tissue necrosis — use large vein
Not recommended for rectal administration in children (unreliable absorption)

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute 500 mg with 20 mL SWFI to give 2.5% (25 mg/mL); for neonates use 1% (10 mg/mL)
administration.dilution: Further dilute in NS for continuous infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Induction: inject over 10-15 seconds; infusion: 1-5 mg/kg/hr via syringe pump
administration.compatibility_note: Highly alkaline (pH 10-11) — do NOT mix with acidic drugs (suxamethonium, vecuronium, atracurium); flush line
administration.storage: Reconstituted solution stable for 24 hours at room temperature; discard if precipitate forms

storage_instructions: Store powder below 25°C; reconstituted solution: use within 24 hours; discard if not clear
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk; single induction dose unlikely to cause significant infant exposure

unlicensed_note: Licensed for >=8 years (some regions >=18 only); used off-label in younger children for MDR infections with no alternative; avoid in <8 years due to teeth discolouration

formulations.indian_conc_note: Lyophilised powder; reconstitute with 5.3 mL NS to give 10 mg/mL, then dilute further

dosing_bands.duration_note: Age 8-11: Loading 1.5 mg/kg IV then 1.2 mg/kg IV q12h; max 50 mg/dose; age 12-17: loading 100 mg then 50 mg q12h
dosing_bands.notes: Reserve for MDR infections (ESBL, CRE) with no alternative; infuse over 30-60 minutes; not effective against Pseudomonas aeruginosa

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading 100 mg IV then 50 mg IV q12h; duration 5-14 days based on infection severity
dosing_bands.notes: Adult dosing for adolescents >=12 years and >=50 kg; not for use in bloodstream infections (higher mortality in trials)

hepatic_note: Child-Pugh C: loading dose 100 mg then reduce maintenance to 25 mg q12h; monitor LFTs closely

interactions.effect: Increases warfarin effect — monitor INR closely and reduce warfarin dose
May reduce efficacy of oral contraceptives

black_box_warnings: Increased mortality risk observed in meta-analysis of clinical trials — reserve for situations where no alternative treatments are available
pediatric_specific_warnings: Avoid in children <8 years — permanent teeth discolouration (yellow-brown)
Nausea and vomiting very common in children (>30%)
Not effective against Pseudomonas — do not use empirically for nosocomial pneumonia
FDA warning: higher mortality in pooled analysis — use only when no alternative

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute 50 mg vial with 5.3 mL NS or D5W to give 10 mg/mL
administration.dilution: Further dilute to 100 mL NS or D5W (final concentration 1 mg/mL or lower)
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS, D5W, RL; do NOT mix with other drugs; Y-site incompatible with amphotericin B
administration.storage: Reconstituted: use within 6 hours at room temperature or 24 hours at 2-8°C

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; reconstituted solution: use within 24 hours if refrigerated
lactation_note: Unknown excretion; avoid due to tetracycline-class effects on nursing infant teeth and bones

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages including neonates (systemic); nebulised form licensed for cystic fibrosis >=6 years; ophthalmic licensed for >=1 year

formulations.indian_conc_note: 40 mg/mL injectable solution
Preservative-free nebuliser solution for cystic fibrosis
3 mg/mL tobramycin ophthalmic solution

dosing_bands.duration_note: Preterm <32 wk: 4-5 mg/kg every 36-48 hours; Term neonate: 5 mg/kg every 24 hours; extended interval dosing preferred
dosing_bands.notes: Extended interval dosing in neonates; monitor trough levels (<2 mcg/mL) and peak (8-12 mcg/mL); renal function monitoring essential

dosing_bands.duration_note: Once daily (extended interval) dosing preferred: 5-7 mg/kg IV OD; or traditional: 2.5 mg/kg IV q8h; duration 7-10 days
dosing_bands.notes: Extended interval dosing superior — higher peak, lower trough, reduced nephrotoxicity; always combine with beta-lactam for synergy

dosing_bands.duration_note: 300 mg nebulised BID for 28 days ON, then 28 days OFF; use PARI LC Plus or equivalent jet nebuliser
dosing_bands.notes: Nebulised — minimal systemic absorption; administer after airway clearance therapy and bronchodilator; for >=6 years

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-2 drops in affected eye every 4-6 hours for 7 days; severe: every 2 hours for first 24-48 hours then reduce
dosing_bands.notes: Ophthalmic use; ointment: apply 1 cm ribbon to lower conjunctival sac 2-3 times daily

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Extend interval to q36h; monitor levels
Extend interval to q48h; monitor levels
Individualize dosing based on serum levels; consider avoidance

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to tobramycin or other aminoglycosides
cross_reactions: Other aminoglycosides (gentamicin, amikacin) — cross-sensitivity and additive toxicity

interactions.drug_or_class: Other nephrotoxic drugs (vancomycin, amphotericin B, NSAIDs)
interactions.effect: Additive nephrotoxicity — monitor renal function daily
Additive ototoxicity — avoid concurrent use if possible
Potentiates neuromuscular blockade — risk of prolonged paralysis

monitoring_parameters: Serum tobramycin levels: trough <2 mcg/mL, peak 8-12 mcg/mL (traditional) or peak 20-30 mcg/mL (extended interval)
black_box_warnings: Nephrotoxicity — may cause irreversible renal impairment; monitor renal function
Ototoxicity — may cause irreversible hearing loss and vestibular damage; risk increases with duration and concurrent ototoxic drugs
Neuromuscular blockade — risk of respiratory paralysis especially with concurrent anaesthetic agents
pediatric_specific_warnings: Neonates: immature renal function prolongs half-life — extended interval dosing mandatory
Monitor hearing in neonates and infants (high-frequency loss may be missed)
Avoid concurrent ototoxic drugs in premature infants
Therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM) essential in all pediatric patients

administration.dilution: Dilute in 50-100 mL NS or D5W (neonates: volume to infuse over 30 min)
administration.infusion_rate_note: Infuse over 20-30 minutes; never give as IV push
administration.compatibility_note: Do NOT mix with beta-lactams in same line (inactivation); Y-site compatible with most common PICU drugs
administration.storage: Store below 25°C; diluted solution stable 24 hours at room temperature

administration.compatibility_note: Wait 5 min between different eye drops
administration.storage: Store below 25°C; discard 4 weeks after opening

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light; nebuliser solution: refrigerate, use immediately after opening
lactation_note: Poorly absorbed from infant GI tract; compatible with breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for children; used off-label in pediatric cardiac and renal oedema; longer duration of action than furosemide

formulations.indian_conc_note: 5 mg/mL injectable solution

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.1-0.4 mg/kg OD; torsemide:furosemide ratio approximately 1:4 (10 mg torsemide ≈ 40 mg furosemide); titrate to effect
dosing_bands.notes: Off-label in children; longer duration and more predictable bioavailability than furosemide; once daily dosing usually sufficient

dosing_bands.duration_note: IV slow push over 2 minutes; oral:IV ratio is 1:1 (equivalent bioavailability)
dosing_bands.notes: IV and oral doses are equivalent (unlike furosemide where IV:PO ratio is 1:2)

hepatic_note: Use with caution in hepatic cirrhosis — risk of hepatic encephalopathy from electrolyte depletion; give with potassium-sparing diuretic

interactions.effect: Additive ototoxicity — avoid concurrent use if possible
Hypokalaemia potentiates digoxin toxicity — monitor K+ closely
May reduce diuretic effect and worsen renal function
Increases lithium levels — risk of toxicity

pediatric_specific_warnings: Limited pediatric data — use furosemide as first-line loop diuretic
Risk of severe electrolyte depletion in children — monitor K+ and Na+ closely
May cause hypercalciuria and nephrocalcinosis in neonates (avoid in neonates)
Dehydration risk higher in children — careful fluid balance monitoring

administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS; avoid mixing with other drugs

food_instructions: May be taken with or without food; take in the morning to avoid nocturia
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; protect from light
lactation_note: Unknown excretion in breast milk; use with caution — may suppress lactation

drug_class: Centrally acting opioid analgesic (weak mu-agonist + SNRI)
unlicensed_note: Not recommended in children <12 years; FDA and EMA restrict use in <18 years for post-tonsillectomy/adenoidectomy pain; use off-label in children >=12 years under specialist supervision only

formulations.indian_conc_note: 50 mg/mL injectable solution
100 mg/mL oral drops

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-2 mg/kg/dose every 6-8 hours as needed; use lowest effective dose for shortest duration; avoid in <12 years unless specialist
dosing_bands.notes: RESTRICTED USE IN CHILDREN: FDA/EMA warnings against use <12 years and <18 years post-tonsillectomy; CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers at risk of fatal respiratory depression; >=12 years only

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-1.5 mg/kg IV slow push over 2-3 min; every 6-8 hours; for >=12 years only; switch to oral ASAP
dosing_bands.notes: IV: slow push over 2-3 minutes to reduce nausea/seizure risk; avoid rapid IV bolus; monitor for respiratory depression

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Extend interval to q12h; avoid in severe renal impairment (GFR <10 — not dialyzable)

hepatic_note: Reduce dose by 50% in cirrhosis; avoid in severe hepatic impairment; metabolism via CYP2D6 and CYP3A4
contraindications: Known or suspected CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers
Acute intoxication with alcohol, opioids, or psychotropic drugs

interactions.effect: Serotonin syndrome — contraindicated within 14 days of MAOI use
Increased risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures
Reduces tramadol efficacy via CYP3A4 induction and increases seizure risk
May reduce tramadol analgesic efficacy

monitoring_parameters: Signs of serotonin syndrome (agitation, tremor, hyperthermia)
black_box_warnings: Risk of fatal respiratory depression in CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers — especially children post-tonsillectomy
Serotonin syndrome risk when combined with serotonergic drugs
Neonatal opioid withdrawal syndrome if used during pregnancy
pediatric_specific_warnings: CONTRAINDICATED in <12 years and for post-tonsillectomy pain in <18 years
CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers at risk of life-threatening respiratory depression — deaths reported in children
Lowers seizure threshold — avoid in children with epilepsy
Not first-line analgesic — use paracetamol/ibuprofen first
Use lowest dose for shortest duration

administration.dilution: May dilute in NS or D5W for infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Slow IV push over 2-3 minutes; or infusion over 15-30 min

food_instructions: May be taken with or without food; take with food if GI upset occurs
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; Schedule H1 controlled substance in India
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk; avoid — risk of sedation and respiratory depression in breastfed infant
reference_source: FDA Safety Communication (2017): Tramadol use in children

unlicensed_note: Widely used in Indian pediatric practice for procedural sedation; preferred over chloral hydrate for better palatability and fewer GI side effects

formulations.indian_conc_note: 100 mg/mL triclofos sodium oral solution

dosing_bands.duration_note: Single dose 50-75 mg/kg PO 30-45 min before procedure; may repeat half dose once if sedation inadequate after 30 min
dosing_bands.notes: Give 30-45 min before procedure; onset 20-40 min, duration 1-2 hours; keep nil by mouth 4 hours prior; monitor SpO2 throughout

dosing_bands.duration_note: Single dose 50-100 mg/kg PO; max 2 g; give 30-45 min before procedure; older children may need higher doses
dosing_bands.notes: Bitter taste — mix with flavoured syrup; paradoxical excitation in ~5% of children; not reliable for >5 years; have rescue equipment ready

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Active metabolite trichloroethanol accumulates — avoid in severe renal impairment

hepatic_note: Avoid in severe hepatic impairment — chloral derivatives are hepatotoxic at high doses

interactions.effect: Transiently increases warfarin effect via protein binding displacement
Additive CNS and respiratory depression — avoid combination or reduce doses
Reports of flushing, diaphoresis, and BP changes when given concurrently

pediatric_specific_warnings: Paradoxical excitation in 5-10% of children — be prepared with alternative sedation
Risk of airway obstruction in deeply sedated children — trained personnel and equipment mandatory
Not reliable in children >5 years — consider midazolam or propofol
Keep child nil by mouth 4 hours before administration
Post-procedure observation until fully alert and protective reflexes returned

administration.dilution: May dilute with fruit juice or flavoured syrup to mask bitter taste

food_instructions: Give on empty stomach (nil by mouth 4 hours prior) for procedures; may mix with juice for palatability
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light; discard if discoloured
lactation_note: Active metabolite excreted in breast milk — sedation risk to infant
reference_source: NICE Guidelines on Sedation in Children

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children and adults; short-acting mydriatic preferred in pediatrics for fundus examination and refraction

formulations.indian_conc_note: 5 mg/mL tropicamide; preferred strength for pediatric fundoscopy
10 mg/mL tropicamide; used for cycloplegia and pre-operative mydriasis

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 drop 0.5% in each eye; repeat after 5 min if needed; for ROP screening combine with phenylephrine 2.5%; examine after 20-30 min
dosing_bands.notes: Use 0.5% ONLY in neonates; systemic absorption risk — press lacrimal punctum for 2 minutes after instillation; watch for feeding intolerance, abdominal distension

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 drop 0.5% or 1% in each eye 15-20 min before examination; may repeat once after 5 min if needed
dosing_bands.notes: Short-acting mydriatic — effect wears off in 4-6 hours; recovery faster than cyclopentolate or atropine; not sufficient for full cycloplegia in young children with brown irides

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 drop 1% repeated after 5 min; refract 20-30 min after last drop; cycloplegia incomplete in children <5 years — use cyclopentolate instead
dosing_bands.notes: Tropicamide provides incomplete cycloplegia in young children — cyclopentolate 1% preferred for accurate cycloplegic refraction

contraindications: Narrow-angle glaucoma (may precipitate acute angle closure)

interactions.effect: Additive anticholinergic effects if systemically absorbed

monitoring_parameters: Systemic anticholinergic effects in neonates (tachycardia, flushing, feeding issues)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Neonates: use 0.5% only; press lacrimal punctum after instillation to minimise systemic absorption
May cause feeding intolerance and abdominal distension in preterm neonates
Transient stinging on instillation — warn parents
Photophobia and blurred vision for 4-6 hours — protect from bright light

administration.compatibility_note: Wait 5 min between different eye drops; compatible with phenylephrine eye drops for enhanced mydriasis
administration.storage: Store below 25°C; discard 4 weeks after opening

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light; discard 4 weeks after opening
lactation_note: Minimal systemic absorption from ophthalmic use; compatible with breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: No specific pediatric license; widely used in Indian practice for post-operative and post-traumatic oedema; limited evidence base

formulations.indian_conc_note: Trypsin 48 mg + Chymotrypsin 2 mg (total 100,000 Armour Units) enteric-coated

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 tablet BD for 5-7 days; take 30 min before meals for optimal absorption; older children only (capable of swallowing enteric-coated tablet)
dosing_bands.notes: Limited evidence; not recommended for young children who cannot swallow enteric-coated tablets; do not crush/chew; generally >8 years only

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to trypsin or chymotrypsin

interactions.effect: May enhance anticoagulant effect — theoretical increase in bleeding risk

pediatric_specific_warnings: Not suitable for children who cannot swallow enteric-coated tablets whole
Do not crush or chew — enzyme activity destroyed by gastric acid
Limited evidence for efficacy — consider NSAIDs as first-line anti-inflammatory
food_instructions: Take 30 minutes before meals on empty stomach for optimal absorption; do NOT crush or chew
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from moisture; keep in original blister pack
lactation_note: Enzymatic protein — unlikely to be systemically absorbed; probably safe during lactation

unlicensed_note: Not specifically licensed for pediatric use; used off-label in children for colicky abdominal pain and smooth muscle spasm; limited pediatric data

formulations.indian_conc_note: 8 mg/mL valethamate bromide
0.4 mg/mL valethamate bromide oral solution

dosing_bands.duration_note: Children 2-6 years: 5 mL (2 mg) TDS; 6-12 years: 10 mL (4 mg) TDS; use syrup formulation
dosing_bands.notes: Off-label; limited pediatric data; use for short duration only; anticholinergic side effects (dry mouth, tachycardia) — caution in hot weather (hyperthermia risk)

interactions.effect: Additive anticholinergic effects — dry mouth, tachycardia, urinary retention
Antagonises prokinetic effect — reduced GI motility

pediatric_specific_warnings: Risk of hyperthermia in hot climate — reduced sweating from anticholinergic effect
May cause paradoxical CNS excitation in young children
Avoid in infants <2 years — increased anticholinergic sensitivity
Short-term use only (3-5 days)
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; protect from light
lactation_note: May reduce milk production due to anticholinergic effect; avoid if possible

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=9 months (some brands >=12 months); IAP recommends routine vaccination at 15 months and 4-6 years

formulations.indian_conc_note: Live attenuated Oka strain varicella-zoster virus >=10^3.3 PFU; reconstitute with provided diluent

dosing_bands.duration_note: Dose 1: 0.5 mL SC at 15 months (IAP); Dose 2: 0.5 mL SC at 4-6 years; minimum interval 3 months between doses
dosing_bands.notes: IAP 2024: 2 doses at 15 months and 4-6 years; can give from 9 months in outbreak settings; minimum interval between doses: 3 months (children <13 yr) or 4-8 weeks (>=13 yr)

dosing_bands.duration_note: Post-exposure: give within 72 hours (ideally) to 5 days of exposure; catch-up: 2 doses 3 months apart
dosing_bands.notes: Post-exposure prophylaxis within 3-5 days of exposure prevents or modifies disease; avoid salicylates for 6 weeks post-vaccination (Reye syndrome risk)

contraindications: Immunodeficiency (primary or acquired — HIV with CD4 <15%)
Pregnancy (avoid pregnancy for 1 month after vaccination)
Anaphylaxis to previous dose or neomycin/gelatin
cross_reactions: Other live vaccines — space by 4 weeks or give simultaneously

interactions.effect: Avoid salicylates for 6 weeks post-vaccination — theoretical risk of Reye syndrome
Defer vaccination for 3-11 months after immunoglobulin (depending on product and dose)
May reduce vaccine efficacy — avoid antivirals for 14 days before and 21 days after vaccination

pediatric_specific_warnings: Live vaccine — do NOT give to immunocompromised children
Avoid salicylates for 6 weeks after vaccination
Vaccine-strain varicella may rarely be transmitted to susceptible contacts — counsel parents
Mild varicella-like rash in 3-5% is expected and self-limiting
Can be given simultaneously with MMR at different sites (or use MMRV)

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute lyophilised vaccine with provided diluent (0.5 mL SWFI); use within 30 min
administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with other vaccines in same syringe; can give simultaneously at different site
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C (or -20°C for long-term); protect from light; use within 30 min of reconstitution

storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C (cold chain) or -20°C for long-term; protect from light; reconstituted vaccine: use within 30 minutes
lactation_note: Vaccine-strain VZV not detected in breast milk in studies; safe during lactation
reference_source: WHO Position Paper on Varicella Vaccines

unlicensed_note: Not specifically licensed for pediatric use; used off-label in PICU for vasodilatory shock, cardiac arrest, and diabetes insipidus; PALS guidelines support use

formulations.indian_conc_note: Arginine vasopressin (AVP) 20 units/mL

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 0.0002-0.0005 units/kg/min continuous infusion; titrate up to max 0.002 units/kg/min; wean slowly once vasopressors reduced
dosing_bands.notes: PICU only; use as adjunct to catecholamines in vasodilatory/septic shock; do NOT use as sole vasopressor; taper over 1-2 hours to avoid rebound hypotension

dosing_bands.duration_note: 40 units IV push (single dose); may replace first or second dose of epinephrine in cardiac arrest; adolescents/adult dose
dosing_bands.notes: AHA/PALS: vasopressin no longer routinely recommended over epinephrine in pediatric cardiac arrest; may be considered in refractory arrest; dose not well established in younger children

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.0005-0.01 units/kg/hr continuous infusion; titrate based on urine output (target 2-3 mL/kg/hr) and serum sodium
dosing_bands.notes: Acute post-neurosurgical DI; transition to desmopressin (DDAVP) once oral intake established; monitor serum Na+ every 2-4 hours

interactions.effect: Potentiates antidiuretic effect — risk of water intoxication and hyponatraemia
Antagonises antidiuretic effect of vasopressin
Additive vasoconstriction — risk of peripheral and visceral ischaemia

monitoring_parameters: Serum sodium (every 2-4 hours — risk of hyponatraemia)
Peripheral perfusion (risk of digital and skin ischaemia)
pediatric_specific_warnings: PICU setting mandatory — continuous haemodynamic monitoring required
Severe risk of water intoxication and hyponatraemia — restrict free water intake
Peripheral and mesenteric ischaemia risk — monitor digits and abdomen
Taper slowly to avoid rebound hypotension
Limited pediatric pharmacokinetic data — dose titration essential

administration.dilution: Dilute in NS or D5W; typical PICU concentration: 0.1-1 units/mL
administration.infusion_rate_note: Continuous infusion via syringe pump; never give concentrated bolus except in cardiac arrest
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with NS and D5W; incompatible with norepinephrine in same line — use separate lumen
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C; diluted solutions stable for 24 hours

storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C; protect from light; diluted infusion: use within 24 hours
lactation_note: Endogenous hormone; exogenous vasopressin destroyed in infant GI tract; compatible with breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages including neonates; widely used intermediate-acting NMBA in pediatric anaesthesia and critical care

formulations.indian_conc_note: Reconstitute with 4 mL SWFI to give 1 mg/mL
Reconstitute with 10 mL SWFI to give 1 mg/mL

dosing_bands.duration_note: Intubating dose 0.1 mg/kg IV; maintenance 0.03-0.05 mg/kg every 15-20 min as needed; neonates are more sensitive — longer duration of action
dosing_bands.notes: Neonates more sensitive than older children; onset 2-3 min, duration 30-40 min; use nerve stimulator (TOF) monitoring

dosing_bands.duration_note: Intubating dose: 0.08-0.1 mg/kg IV; maintenance: 0.01-0.02 mg/kg every 15-20 min PRN; or infusion 0.05-0.1 mg/kg/hr
dosing_bands.notes: Onset 2-3 min; duration 20-35 min in children (shorter than adults); children require relatively higher doses than adults on weight basis

dosing_bands.duration_note: Continuous infusion 0.05-0.1 mg/kg/hr; titrate with TOF monitoring to maintain 1-2/4 twitches; always combine with adequate sedation and analgesia
dosing_bands.notes: PICU: mandatory concurrent sedation and analgesia; daily drug holidays for neurological assessment; risk of ICU-acquired weakness with prolonged use; TOF monitoring essential

hepatic_note: Primarily hepatic elimination; prolonged duration in severe hepatic disease — use with TOF monitoring; consider atracurium as alternative
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to vecuronium or bromides

interactions.effect: Potentiates and prolongs neuromuscular blockade — may cause prolonged paralysis
Potentiates vecuronium — reduce vecuronium dose by 25-40%
Significantly potentiates neuromuscular blockade — reduce vecuronium dose
Rapid reversal of vecuronium blockade — use for emergency reversal

monitoring_parameters: Adequacy of sedation and analgesia (paralysed patient cannot express pain)
black_box_warnings: Paralysis without adequate sedation causes awareness and extreme psychological distress — ALWAYS ensure concurrent sedation and analgesia
pediatric_specific_warnings: NEVER administer without secure airway and ability to ventilate
Must combine with adequate sedation and analgesia — paralysed child cannot communicate distress
Neonates: more sensitive, longer duration — use lower doses
Children: may need relatively higher doses per kg than adults
Prolonged use in PICU causes ICU-acquired weakness — daily drug holidays recommended

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute with SWFI: 4 mg in 4 mL or 10 mg in 10 mL (1 mg/mL)
administration.dilution: For infusion: further dilute to 0.1-0.2 mg/mL in NS or D5W
administration.infusion_rate_note: Bolus: rapid IV push; Infusion: 0.05-0.1 mg/kg/hr via syringe pump
administration.compatibility_note: Physically incompatible with thiopental (precipitates) — flush line; compatible with NS, D5W, RL
administration.storage: Reconstituted solution: refrigerate, use within 24 hours; or 12 hours at room temperature

storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C; reconstituted solution: use within 24 hours refrigerated or 12 hours at room temperature
lactation_note: Quaternary ammonium compound — poorly absorbed orally; minimal risk to breastfed infant

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages; used as diluent for reconstitution and dilution of parenteral medications

formulations.indian_conc_note: Sterile, pyrogen-free water for injection; NOT for direct IV infusion
For use in pharmacy compounding; NOT for direct infusion

dosing_bands.duration_note: Use as directed for specific drug reconstitution; volume per drug manufacturer instructions; NEVER infuse pure WFI — always add solute first
dosing_bands.notes: CRITICAL: Never infuse pure water for injection — causes haemolysis due to hypotonicity; always use as diluent for medications; for neonates: use smallest possible volumes to avoid fluid overload

contraindications: NEVER administer undiluted as IV infusion — causes haemolysis, renal failure, and death
Not a substitute for Normal Saline or other isotonic solutions
monitoring_parameters: Not applicable as standalone agent; monitor reconstituted drug parameters
black_box_warnings: Fatal if infused undiluted IV — causes acute intravascular haemolysis, hyperkalaemia, and death; must only be used as a vehicle for reconstitution/dilution
pediatric_specific_warnings: NEVER infuse pure WFI — fatal haemolysis risk especially in neonates
Neonates and infants: minimise free water volume to prevent hyponatraemia and fluid overload
Always verify correct diluent for each drug — some drugs require NS or D5W, not WFI
Single-use ampoules — discard unused portion

administration.reconstitution: Used as the reconstitution vehicle itself — follow specific drug instructions
administration.infusion_rate_note: NEVER infuse undiluted; used only to reconstitute/dilute other medications
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with most injectable medications; verify specific drug compatibility
administration.storage: Store below 25°C; single-use — discard unused portion

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; single-use containers — discard after opening; do not use if particulate matter visible
lactation_note: Not applicable — pharmaceutical vehicle

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages; commonly used in pediatric dry eye, post-ocular surgery, and in children with reduced blink rate (screen use, neurological conditions)

formulations.indian_conc_note: Carboxymethylcellulose sodium 5 mg/mL; preserved with purite (oxychloro complex)
Preservative-free single-use vials; preferred for frequent use and in neonates/infants
Higher viscosity gel drops for moderate-severe dry eye

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 drop in each affected eye 3-4 times daily or as needed; use preservative-free formulation in infants and neonates
dosing_bands.notes: Preservative-free unit-dose preferred for infants; no systemic absorption; safe for frequent use; discard unit-dose vial after single use

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-2 drops in each affected eye 3-4 times daily or as needed; may increase frequency in severe dry eye; no maximum daily limit
dosing_bands.notes: Safe for long-term use; preservative-free preferred if using >4 times daily; may be used before/after contact lens use; 0.5% for mild, 1% for moderate-severe

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to carboxymethylcellulose or preservatives in the formulation

interactions.effect: Wait 5-10 minutes between different eye drops; instil CMC drops last as viscous formulations may delay absorption of other drops

monitoring_parameters: Symptom improvement (dryness, irritation, foreign body sensation)
Ocular surface assessment (tear break-up time, Schirmer test)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Use preservative-free formulation in neonates, infants, and when using >4 times daily
Temporary blurring of vision after instillation — supervise young children
Ensure child does not rub eyes after instillation

administration.compatibility_note: Wait 5-10 min between other eye drops; instil CMC drops last if using multiple eye medications
administration.storage: Store below 25°C; multi-dose: discard 4 weeks after opening; unit-dose: single use

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; multi-dose bottle: discard 4 weeks after opening; unit-dose vials: single use, discard after use
lactation_note: No systemic absorption; safe during lactation

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children and adults; widely used in pediatric ENT for otitis externa; otic formulation preferred for ear infections — avoid ophthalmic use (neomycin ototoxicity risk with perforated TM)

formulations.indian_conc_note: Neomycin 3.5 mg + Polymyxin B 10,000 U + Bacitracin 400 U + Hydrocortisone 10 mg per mL
Neomycin 3.5 mg + Polymyxin B 5,000 U + Bacitracin 400 U + Hydrocortisone 10 mg per g

dosing_bands.duration_note: 3-4 drops in affected ear TDS-QDS for 7-10 days; warm drops to body temperature before instillation; keep ear drops in ear for 5 min (tragal pump)
dosing_bands.notes: CONFIRM intact tympanic membrane before use — neomycin is ototoxic if enters middle ear through perforation; if TM status unknown, use ciprofloxacin ear drops instead

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply thin layer to affected area 2-3 times daily for 7 days; do not use under occlusion; avoid large areas or broken skin (systemic absorption risk)
dosing_bands.notes: Short-term use only (7-10 days); avoid on face/genitals (steroid atrophy); neomycin contact allergy common — switch if rash worsens; do not use under occlusive dressings

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to neomycin or other aminoglycosides
Viral infections (herpes, varicella) — steroid may worsen
Fungal skin infections (unless combined with antifungal)
cross_reactions: Contact dermatitis to neomycin (common — up to 6% of treated patients)

interactions.effect: Additive ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity if significant systemic absorption from large wounds

monitoring_parameters: Signs of contact allergy (worsening rash, pruritus — neomycin allergy)
Hearing (if prolonged ear use or perforated TM suspected)
pediatric_specific_warnings: ALWAYS confirm intact tympanic membrane before otic use — neomycin causes sensorineural hearing loss if it reaches middle ear
Limit duration to 7-10 days — prolonged steroid use causes skin atrophy
Neomycin contact allergy is common — if rash worsens, suspect allergy and discontinue
Avoid on large body surface area in infants — risk of systemic aminoglycoside absorption
Do not use ophthalmic — neomycin is toxic to corneal epithelium with prolonged use

administration.storage: Store below 25°C; discard 4 weeks after opening

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; discard ear drops 4 weeks after opening; ointment: use within 6 months of opening
lactation_note: Minimal systemic absorption from topical/otic use; safe during lactation

drug_class: Super-potent corticosteroid + aminoglycoside antibiotic + azole antifungal combination (topical)
unlicensed_note: Clobetasol is a super-potent (Group I) steroid — NOT recommended for routine pediatric use; use only under dermatology specialist guidance for short duration; widely misused in India for non-specific dermatoses

formulations.indian_conc_note: Clobetasol propionate 0.05% + Neomycin sulphate 0.5% + Miconazole nitrate 2% w/w
Clobetasol propionate 0.05% + Neomycin sulphate 0.5% + Miconazole nitrate 2% w/w

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply thin layer to affected area BD for maximum 7 days; do NOT use under occlusion; avoid face, genitals, flexures; specialist use only in children
dosing_bands.notes: SUPER-POTENT steroid — NOT for routine pediatric use; maximum 7 days in children; limit to <10% BSA; risk of HPA axis suppression, skin atrophy, striae; NEVER use on face or diaper area; prefer milder steroid combinations (hydrocortisone/mometasone) as first-line

contraindications: Primary viral (herpes, varicella), bacterial, or fungal skin infections (monotherapy)

interactions.effect: Additive skin atrophy risk if used on overlapping areas

monitoring_parameters: Signs of skin atrophy (thinning, telangiectasia, striae)
HPA axis suppression if used on large areas or under occlusion in children (morning cortisol)
pediatric_specific_warnings: SUPER-POTENT (Group I) steroid — children are at higher risk of systemic absorption due to higher BSA:weight ratio
NEVER use on face, genitals, flexures, or diaper area
Maximum duration 7 days in children — risk of irreversible skin atrophy and striae
HPA axis suppression possible even with short-term use on large areas
Avoid under occlusion (diapers act as occlusive dressings)
First-line: use hydrocortisone 1% or mometasone 0.1% based combinations instead
Widely misused in India — educate parents about steroid skin damage
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; do not freeze; close cap tightly after use
lactation_note: Avoid application on breast area; clobetasol is super-potent — systemic absorption risk
reference_source: Indian Association of Dermatologists (IADVL) Advisory on Irrational FDC Topicals
